warnings: Section 2.8 yielded no reusable atomic records after chapter-level review.
Priority and causal claims were omitted unless explicitly stated and attributed.
The IBM 407 was discussed in the DMATS computational context, but no claim that DMATS used that exact model was retained.
Chapter-outline sentences in section 4.1 were mostly skipped unless they directly supported model, method, or work relationships.
Bracketed author-year citations in section 4.1 were not registered as external sources or enriched with bibliographic metadata because the external works were not inspected in this run.
No claim was retained for the 'golden age' metaphor because it would require a broader historical interpretation not needed for the graph.
The possible full-name correction from Beesley to Michael Beesley was handled as an alias merge only; the existing canonical entity name was left unchanged.
Changed an unresolved place_id qualifier to a plain place string for the section 4.1 Andrew Daly/value-of-time claim.
Wikipedia references in notes 2, 4, 5, 6, and 16 were not registered as external sources because they were not inspected in this run.
Several cited works were left as bracketed citations rather than Work entities where no retained claim required a separate node.

The chapter's interpretive statement that MNL became dominant was not converted to a factual dominance claim; only the explicit statement that MNL was the main empirical tool in this period was retained.
Figure 4.2 and Figure 4.3 image contents were not transcribed into table-like claims because the Markdown source stores them as images without stable textual rows.
Spear's evaluative statements were retained as attributed views rather than unqualified facts.
No external bibliographic metadata was added for Spear 1977, Peat Marwick 1972, Rassam et al 1971, or Train 1978 because those works were not inspected.
Figures 4.4 and 4.5 were not transcribed into claims because their visual tree details are image-only in the Markdown source.
The authors' statement that CRA 1972 was the single most important contribution was not retained as a factual ranking claim.
Bibliographic details for CRA 1972, Domencich and McFadden 1975, Ben-Akiva 1973/1974, and related works were not enriched from outside sources.
No external source records were created for Richards and Ben-Akiva 1975, Coopers and Lybrand Associates 1973, Cochrane 1975, Koenig 1975, or Harris and Tanner 1974 because they were not inspected.
Detailed route and freight survey variables were summarized only where they directly supported retained model claims.
The broader historical importance of Harris and Tanner 1974 was not asserted beyond the source's attributed description and concrete technical claims.
Figure 4.6 was not transcribed because its structures are image-only in the Markdown source.
McFadden's prescriptive advice to practitioners was retained as an attributed view.
No external details were added for CRA 1976, McFadden et al 1977b, Train 1978, Brand 1973, or Liou and Talvitie 1974.
General retrospective sentiments about optimism, mixed acceptance, and being at the challenge of the times were not retained unless attached to a named attributed view.
No external source records were created for McFadden 1976b, Tversky 1972, Stopher and Meyburg 1975, or Spear 1977.
The final sentence pointing to Chapter 5 was not extracted.
Wikipedia URLs in notes 2, 4, 5, 6, and 16 were not registered as external sources because they were not inspected.
Most bibliographic pointers in notes 8, 9, 13, 15, 18, 27, and 28 were not converted into Work entities because no retained claim required them.
Personal communication notes were treated cautiously as source-book evidence only, with lower confidence and attributed_view where retained.
